professional_persona: Rajaram Agand is a diligent railway guard who excels at safety monitoring, crowd control, and first aid, applying his practical mindset and curious problem‑solving to keep trains on schedule while they offer calm reassurance to anxious passengers.
linguistic_persona: Rajaram Agand speaks Marathi fluently, reads and writes it at a functional level, comfortably communicates in Hindi, and relies on basic English signage for occasional instructions, which they navigate with practical ease.
sports_persona: Rajaram Agand is a passionate follower of cricket, cheering for the Mumbai Indians and the Maharashtra Ranji team, while they join a local Sunday cricket gathering in their village field to stay active.
arts_persona: Rajaram Agand enjoys Marathi folk music by Shahir Sable and bhajans by Anup Jalota, often humming while photographing passing trains, and they unwind by watching classic Marathi cinema like 'Sairat'.
travel_persona: Rajaram Agand prefers short train trips to nearby hill stations like Lonavala and Mahabaleshwar, often planning weekend getaways on a modest budget, while they dream of a pilgrimage to Shirdi and an adventurous long‑haul journey to Kashmir.
culinary_persona: Rajaram Agand enjoys hearty Maharashtrian dishes such as jowar bhakri with pithla, misal pav, and steaming hot amti, relishing flavors of fresh coriander, ginger, and peanuts, though they rarely cook and prefer dining at home with family.
persona: Rajaram Agand is a 31‑year‑old railway guard whose practical, curious mind keeps trains on time while they let their intense emotions drive both disciplined frugality and occasional impulsive splurges on comforts like a new TV.
cultural_background: Rajaram grew up in the agrarian hinterland of Ahmednagar, a rural Maharashtrian setting where Marathi language, Hindu customs and community values shape daily life. His family participates in festivals such as Ganesh Chaturthi, Gudi Padwa and local village fairs, and they balance reverence for tradition with a cautious curiosity about modern conveniences like mobile ticketing. The cultural fabric of his upbringing emphasizes respect for elders, cooperative work, and a practical approach to problem‑solving.
linguistic_background: Rajaram's mother tongue is Marathi. He completed primary school, so he can read and write Marathi at a functional level and speak it fluently. He also communicates comfortably in Hindi, which he uses to interact with passengers from across India, and can read Hindi script. His formal English exposure is minimal, limited to basic railway signage and occasional instructions, and he relies on Marathi and Hindi for most written communication.
skills_and_expertise: Rajaram excels in railway safety monitoring, passenger assistance, crowd control during peak travel hours, and basic first aid. His role as a guard demands strict adherence to timetables, accurate record‑keeping, and the ability to troubleshoot minor equipment issues. He communicates effectively in both Marathi and Hindi, ensuring clear instructions for passengers. His practical mindset helps him devise quick solutions under pressure, while his curiosity drives him to learn newer safety protocols.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Railway safety monitoring', 'Passenger assistance', 'Crowd control', 'Basic first aid', 'Bilingual communication (Marathi & Hindi)', 'Timekeeping', 'Basic mechanical troubleshooting', 'Record keeping', 'Emergency response']
hobbies_and_interests: Rajaram enjoys listening to Marathi folk songs and bhajans, which help soothe his heightened emotions. He is an avid cricket fan and follows matches on his old television set. In his free time, he tends a small vegetable garden near his home, takes walks through the fields, and occasionally captures photographs of passing trains. He also likes reading short devotional passages from the Bhagavad Gita or local saints, and practices simple breathing exercises for anxiety relief.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Marathi folk songs', 'Singing bhajans', 'Watching cricket', 'Gardening (vegetables)', 'Walking in fields', 'Train photography', 'Reading short devotional passages', 'Practicing breathing exercises']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Rajaram aspires to climb the Indian Railways hierarchy, aiming to become a Senior Guard or Train Conductor within the next five years. He plans to enroll in railway-provided short courses on advanced safety, first aid, and basic electrical maintenance to strengthen his qualifications. Securing a higher post would provide a steadier income, enabling him to support his wife and future children's education. He also wishes to improve his literacy beyond primary level, hoping to read more Hindi literature and possibly learn basic English for better communication.
persona_clinical_summary: Rajaram Agand is a 31‑year‑old male railway guard in rural Maharashtra, currently married, with primary education and a functional Marathi-speaking background. He works in a disciplined yet emotionally expressive role, balancing practical problem-solving with occasional impulsive spending. His lifestyle includes short train trips, occasional cricket viewing, and a preference for traditional Maharashtrian cuisine.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 31
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Guard, Passenger Train
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Ahmadnagar
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: Rajaram Agand is a 31‑year‑old male railway guard in rural Maharashtra, currently married, with primary education and a functional Marathi-speaking background. He works in a disciplined yet emotionally expressive role, balancing practical problem-solving with occasional impulsive spending. His lifestyle includes short train trips, occasional cricket viewing, and a preference for traditional Maharashtrian cuisine.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
इमेजिंग अहवाल — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Ahmadnagar]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Rajaram Agand]] [[AGE|31]] [[GENDER|Male]]
एमआरएन [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] एन्काउंटर [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0915-001]] | निष्कर्ष: रुग्णावर केलेल्या चेस्ट एक्स-रे मध्ये द्विपक्षीय वरच्या लोबमध्ये इन्फिल्ट्रेट्ससह कॅव्हिटेशन दिसून येते, जे सक्रिय फुफ्फुसीय क्षयरोगाशी सुसंगत आहे. प्लुरल इफ्यूजन नोंदवले गेले नाही. पृष्ठीय मणक्यामध्ये स्क्लेरोटिक जखमा दिसतात, जे पॉट्स आजाराचे सूचक आहेत. रुग्णाला क्षयरोगविरोधी उपचार सुरू करण्याचा सल्ला देण्यात आला. हा अहवाल डॉ. प्रकाश देशमुखांनी लिहून घेतला आहे. रुग्णाचा एबीएचए आयडी [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] आहे आणि त्यांचा संपर्क क्रमांक [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] आहे. या केससाठी रुग्णालयाचा आयडी [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MH-001]] आहे. रुग्ण [[VILLAGE|Ralegan]] क्षेत्रातील [[DISTRICT|Ahmadnagar]] जिल्ह्यामध्ये राहतात.
generated_text_en:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Ahmadnagar]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Rajaram Agand]] [[AGE|31]] [[GENDER|Male]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0915-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0915-001]] | Findings: Chest X-ray performed on the patient reveals bilateral upper lobe infiltrates with cavitation, consistent with active pulmonary tuberculosis. No pleural effusion noted. Sclerotic lesions are seen in the dorsal vertebrae, suggestive of Pott's disease. The patient was advised to start anti-tubercular therapy. The report was dictated by Dr. Prakash Deshmukh. The patient's ABHA ID is [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] and his contact number is [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. The hospital ID for this case is [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-MH-001]]. The patient resides in the [[VILLAGE|Ralegan]] area of [[DISTRICT|Ahmadnagar]] district.
judge_reasoning: Marathi Devanagari prose with allowed Latin IDs/tags; all TYPEs in allow-list; male 31yo fits TB/Pott's; concise report, no mismatches.
